meta.purpose: Recommended port, interface and IO baseline for IoTEdges self-developed product models.
meta.important_note: Channel counts and signal ratings are planning targets. Final values must be validated by schematic design, PCB layout, component selection, enclosure constraints, firmware and prototype testing.

io_terms.DI: Digital input for dry contact, wet contact or pulse/status signals depending on circuit design.
io_terms.DO: Digital output, usually transistor output or relay-controlled output depending on model.
io_terms.AI: Analog input, commonly 4-20mA and/or 0-10V for sensors such as pressure, level, flow and temperature transmitters.
io_terms.AO: Analog output, commonly 0-10V or 4-20mA for setpoint/control output; useful but not necessary on every first-generation model.
io_terms.Relay: Mechanical relay output for alarm, switching or control signals; ratings must be validated before publication.
io_terms.RS485: Industrial serial interface commonly used for Modbus RTU meters, sensors, VFDs, inverters and controllers.
io_terms.Ethernet: Wired uplink/configuration interface; can support Modbus TCP, MQTT and web UI depending on firmware.

global_design_rules: Gateway models should prioritize RS485, Ethernet and protocol conversion; built-in IO should be light or optional.
RTU models should prioritize DI, DO/relay, AI and RS485 because IO is the main buying criterion.
Remote IO models should start with separate IO-type variants rather than one overloaded SKU.
AO is useful for control applications but should be a selected Remote IO or advanced RTU variant, not a baseline requirement for every product.
LoRa Remote IO should use fewer IO channels than wired Remote IO because power budget, enclosure size and wireless payload size matter.
Publish exact channel counts only after hardware validation.

model_io_baseline.positioning_note: Keep this as a protocol gateway. Do not make IO the main selling point.

configuration: Web UI over Ethernet or WiFi setup mode

model_io_baseline.positioning_note: WiFi version of the baseline Modbus/MQTT gateway for indoor LAN environments.
Remote protocol gateway. Keep IO light so it does not overlap too much with 4G RTU.
Edge gateway may include light IO for machine status and local rules, but heavy IO should remain RTU/Remote IO territory.
Wired RTU for LAN/SCADA cabinets and local industrial telemetry.

configuration: Web UI over WiFi setup mode or Ethernet
optional: external WiFi antenna depending on enclosure

model_io_baseline.positioning_note: WiFi RTU for indoor facilities where wireless LAN is available.
Main remote telemetry RTU for pump, tank, pipeline, generator and telecom cabinet monitoring.
Higher-channel RTU for wastewater, substations, utility cabinets and multi-sensor field sites.

configuration: DIP switch/address setting or software configuration

model_io_baseline.positioning_note: Remote IO should be modular by IO type; this supports many SEO pages around DI module, relay output module and analog input module.
Keep gateway focused on LoRaWAN network/application integration. Field IO belongs to LoRa nodes.

wireless: LoRa antenna connector or embedded antenna depending on enclosure
power: battery/solar variant only after power budget validation

model_io_baseline.positioning_note: Low-channel wireless IO node for distributed sensors and alarms.

safe_to_say_now: IoTEdges is planning RTU and Remote IO models around DI, DO, AI, AO, relay output, RS485 and Ethernet use cases.
Gateway models should be positioned mainly around RS485, Ethernet, Modbus and MQTT rather than heavy built-in IO.
IEIO-100 will start with separate IO-type variants: DI8, DO8, AI4 and AO4.

next_actions: Use this matrix to create engineering requirement documents for IER-100, IER-140 and IEIO-100 first.
Use IEIO-100-DI8, IEIO-100-DO8, IEIO-100-AI4 and IEIO-100-AO4 as first-generation Remote IO variants.
Use the SEO page mapping to prioritize Remote IO and RTU IO product pages.
